title: feat: upgrade minimax from M2.1 to M2.5 on Fireworks
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: MiniMax levels up
gist.blurb: We've swapped the engine in our MiniMax integration. Version 2.5 is here via Fireworks AI—smarter, faster, and with significantly cheaper cached inputs.
gist.summary: Upgraded the MiniMax text generation model from version 2.1 to 2.5, updating registry configurations and reducing cached input pricing while maintaining legacy access.
gist.impact: Users will automatically experience the improved M2.5 model capabilities and lower costs, though the previous version remains accessible for backward compatibility.
gist.visual_concept: The upgrade is represented by a shiny new component being installed. The 'Fireworks' provider is referenced literally with fireworks in the background. Polly and the Robot are the engineers making the switch.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the bee mascot and the monitor robot at a workbench, installing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) upgrade chip into a retro console. Through the window, pixelated fireworks bloom in the night sky. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites.
